perturbation.original_text: PRODUCTS: The CircuiTest 2000S In-Circuit Test System The CircuiTest 2100 Scanner Expansion
perturbation.changed_text: PRODUCTS: The CircuiTest 2000S In-Circuit Test System The CircuiTest 2100 Scanner Expansion All related software and documentation
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'All related software and documentation' to the definition of 'PRODUCTS' in Section 2, we create a contradiction. Section 9 states 'ITS will supply with each of the Products ordered one set of user and technical documentation and one set of software in reproducible form.' This wording implies that the documentation and software are supplied *with* the products, but are distinct from the definition of the Products themselves. The change now implies the documentation and software *are* the products, creating uncertainty about what COMWARE is actually purchasing when ordering the 'Products'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DOCUMENTATION AND SUPPORT: ITS will supply with each of the Products ordered one set of user and technical documentation and one set of software in reproducible form. COMWARE may purchase additional copies of the documentation and software disks at then prevailing prices. COMWARE will receive minimal assistance and support from ITS consistent with the certifications in Section 1. 3 TERMINATION: except as specifically described elsewhere in this agreement, either ITS or COMWARE shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with 30 days written notice from the other party, for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of 'PRODUCTS' in Section 2 now includes 'All related software and documentation.' This directly contradicts the original wording of the 'DOCUMENTATION AND SUPPORT' section, which states that ITS will supply software and documentation 'with each of the Products ordered,' implying they are separate from the products themselves. The change introduces ambiguity as to whether COMWARE is buying hardware products, software, and documentation, or if the software and documentation are now considered to *be* the products. This inconsistency creates a strong, in-text contradiction that could lead to confusion and disputes regarding the scope of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: WARRANTY: ITS Products are warranted free from defects of material or workmanship for 3 years after shipment from the manufacturer. Equipment purchased from ITS, which becomes defective within that time period will be repaired by ITS at its headquarters in San Antonio, Texas at no cost to COMWARE beyond cost of shipping the equipment to ITS. ITS will bear the cost of returning the item to COMWARE by UPS, REGULAR DELIVERY. priority shipping costs will be borne by COMWARE. This warranty is contingent upon proper use and installation of the Products and does not cover equipment which has been modified without ITS' consent or which has been subjected to unusual physical or electrical stress or on which the original identification marks have been removed or altered.
perturbation.changed_text: WARRANTY: ITS Products are warranted free from defects of material or workmanship for 3 years after shipment from the manufacturer. Equipment purchased from ITS, which becomes defective within that time period will be either repaired or replaced by ITS at its discretion at its headquarters in San Antonio, Texas at no cost to COMWARE beyond cost of shipping the equipment to ITS. ITS will bear the cost of returning the item to COMWARE by UPS, REGULAR DELIVERY unless a replacement is provided. Priority shipping costs will be borne by COMWARE. This warranty is contingent upon proper use and installation of the Products and does not cover equipment which has been modified without ITS' consent or which has been subjected to unusual physical or electrical stress or on which the original identification marks have been removed or altered.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'either repaired or replaced by ITS at its discretion' we create a contradiction with the original text. Originally, the section explicitly stated the faulty 'Equipment purchased from ITS...will be repaired by ITS'. By changing the term to say 'repaired or replaced' its creating an ambiguity and puts ITS in control of that discretion, where under the original text the only path was 'repaired'. This creates confusion, as it's no longer clear if COMWARE has a right to a repair rather than a replacement and uncertainty arises. In the same section the term 'unless a replacement is provided' now forces COMWARE to pay for shipping if they are receiving a replacement instead of it being repaired.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WARRANTY: ITS Products are warranted free from defects of material or workmanship for 3 years after shipment from the manufacturer. Equipment purchased from ITS, which becomes defective within that time period will be either repaired or replaced by ITS at its discretion at its headquarters in San Antonio, Texas at no cost to COMWARE beyond cost of shipping the equipment to ITS. ITS will bear the cost of returning the item to COMWARE by UPS, REGULAR DELIVERY unless a replacement is provided. priority shipping costs will be borne by COMWARE. This warranty is contingent upon proper use and installation of the Products and does not cover equipment which has been modified without ITS' consent or which has been subjected to unusual physical or electrical stress or on which the original identification marks have been removed or altered.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction by changing the warranty terms. Originally, the equipment 'will be repaired'. The changed text states it 'will be either repaired or replaced by ITS at its discretion'. This introduces an ambiguity and gives ITS control over whether to repair or replace, conflicting with the original mandatory repair obligation. Additionally, the phrase 'unless a replacement is provided' shifts shipping costs to COMWARE for replacements, contradicting the initial promise of no cost beyond shipping to ITS, thus creating a financial contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as it introduces inconsistent obligations.

perturbation.original_text: COMWARE will receive minimal assistance and support from ITS consistent with the certifications in Section 1. 3 TERMINATION: except as specifically described elsewhere in this agreement, either ITS or COMWARE shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with 30 days written notice from the other party, for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: COMWARE will receive prioritized assistance and support from ITS consistent with the certifications in Section 1. 3 TERMINATION: except as specifically described elsewhere in this agreement, either ITS or COMWARE shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with 30 days written notice from the other party, for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'minimal assistance' to 'prioritized assistance' a contradiction is created. This change directly contradicts the original intention, especially when considering Section 1 states: 'COMWARE also represents that it is an experienced user of computer equipment and software sufficiently like the Products...and that it needs only minor support'. Changing Section 9 to prioritized support contradicts the original spirit and letter of Section 1, creating an internal contradiction about the level of support COMWARE should expect, and how experienced COMWARE really is.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CERTIFICATION AND IDENTIFICATION: COMWARE TECHNICAL SERVICES OF 17922 SKY PARK CIRCLE, SUITE E, IRVINE, CALIFORNIA 92614-6414 (COMWARE) certifies that the Products furnished under this agreement by INTERNATIONAL TEST SYSTEMS, INC. OF 4703 SHAVANO OAK, SUITE 102, SAN ANTONIO, TEXAS 78249 (ITS) will be incorporated by COMWARE as a part of COMWARE'S systems which COMWARE sells or leases to unaffiliated third parties in the regular course of COMWARE'S business and that COMWARE'S own contribution to these systems reflect a verifiable value added. COMWARE also represents that it is an experienced user of computer equipment and software sufficiently like the Products, as defined below, and that it needs only minor support in the incorporation of the Products into its lines of business.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'minimal assistance' to 'prioritized assistance' in Section 9 directly contradicts the statement in Section 1 that 'COMWARE... needs only minor support'. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the level of support COMWARE requires, impacting the interpretation of COMWARE's expertise and the obligations of ITS, as defined by the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.
